_README: Canonical data layer for the 3D-POLE-Modeler viewers. Human DNA polymerase epsilon catalytic subunit (POLE / POLE1). Every domain, residue, metal site and variant record carries an explicit `sources` list (keys into `references`) and a `verification_status`. STATUS VALUES: 'verified' = confirmed against the cited primary/authoritative source during curation; 'estimated' = inferred (e.g. from yeast structural homology) and NOT yet confirmed in the human record; 'schematic' = the current viewer's illustrative value, retained as a placeholder pending coordinate-derived replacement; 'todo' = must be filled at build time from the named query. Do NOT treat 'estimated' or 'schematic' numbers as authoritative. All residue positions are 1-based on the UniProt Q07864 canonical sequence (2286 aa) unless `numbering_basis` says otherwise.

meta.generated_by: Curation draft for 3D-POLE-Modeler; review before production use.
meta.protein_name: DNA polymerase epsilon catalytic subunit A
meta._numbering_warning: Most POLE structural/functional literature is yeast Pol2 (S. cerevisiae). In the proofreading region the human<->S.cerevisiae offset is +15 (human P286 = yeast P301; human D275/E277 = yeast D290/E292). Offsets are NOT constant across the protein because of indels. Never copy a yeast residue number into a human-labelled field without conversion + verification.

uniprot_q07864.title: UniProtKB: DNA polymerase epsilon catalytic subunit A (POLE), Homo sapiens
uniprot_q07864.note: Release NOT pinned in this draft. Pin the UniProt release (e.g. 2026_xx) at build and re-pull active-site / binding-site feature coordinates from the machine-readable record (REST .txt/.json or EBI Proteins API).

clinvar.title: NCBI ClinVar
clinvar.note: Classifications are LIVE and change. Snapshot the ClinVar release date at build and store it in meta. Both axes (germline classification AND oncogenicity/somatic classification) should be captured where present.

atlas_pole.title: Atlas of Genetics and Cytogenetics in Oncology and Haematology - POLE

roske_yeeles_2024.citation: Roske JJ, Yeeles JTP. Nat Struct Mol Biol 31:1921 (2024).
roske_yeeles_2024.note: PRIMARY HUMAN structural source. Pol epsilon catalytic core with DNA; captures fingers-domain open / ajar / closed states. Use 9F6D coordinates + DSSP to derive all sub-domain boundaries and active-site geometry.

hogg_2014.citation: Hogg M, Osterman P, Bylund GO, et al. Nat Struct Mol Biol 21:49 (2014).
hogg_2014.note: Yeast Pol2 catalytic core; original palm/fingers/thumb/exo/NTD + P-domain description. YEAST numbering.

yuan_2020.citation: Yuan Z, Georgescu R, Schauer GD, et al. Nat Commun 11:3156 (2020).
yuan_2020.note: Holoenzyme architecture; defines the non-catalytic 'Pol2 module' (inactive B-family fold) C-terminal to the active core.

terbeek_2019.citation: Ter Beek J, Parkash V, Bylund GO, et al. Nucleic Acids Res 47:5712 (2019).
terbeek_2019.note: Structural evidence for [4Fe-4S] in the CysX motif within the catalytic core; ternary complex with DNA + dATP.

jain_2014.citation: Jain R, et al. An iron-sulfur cluster in the polymerase domain of yeast DNA polymerase epsilon (2014).
jain_2014.note: Discovery of CysX motif coordinating [4Fe-4S] in the polymerase domain of Pol2.

hertzog_2022.citation: Hertzog M, et al. The iron-sulfur cluster is essential for DNA binding by human DNA polymerase epsilon. Sci Rep 12 (2022).
hertzog_2022.note: Human PolεCD contains the [4Fe-4S] cluster at the base of the P-domain (CysX); cluster-coordinating Cys mutations abolish DNA binding, polymerase and exonuclease activity.

netz_2012.citation: Netz DJA, et al. Nat Chem Biol 8:125 (2012).
netz_2012.note: Original assignment of an essential [4Fe-4S] to the CysB motif (CTD) of B-family polymerases. Superseded for Pol epsilon's catalytic-core cluster by the CysX assignment; CysA/CysB shown to bind Zn in the human p59-p261 crystal.

kane_shcherbakova_2014.citation: Kane DP, Shcherbakova PV. Cancer Res 74:1895 (2014).
kane_shcherbakova_2014.note: P286R (yeast P301R) is an exceptionally strong mutator via a fidelity defect DISTINCT from loss of proofreading; ~50x stronger than exonuclease-null.

parkash_2019.citation: Parkash V, et al. Nat Commun (2019) - structure of Pol epsilon-P301R.
parkash_2019.note: R301 points into the exonuclease site and blocks nascent-strand access; mechanism distinct from cleanexo-inactivation.

palles_2013.citation: Palles C, et al. Nat Genet 45:136 (2013).
palles_2013.note: Defined polymerase-proofreading-associated polyposis (PPAP) from germline exonuclease-domain POLE/POLD1 variants.

elsayed_2015.citation: Elsayed FA, et al. Eur J Hum Genet (2015).
elsayed_2015.note: L424V in familial/early-onset CRC and polyposis; one de novo case.

mur_2023.citation: Mur P, et al. Genome Med 15:85 (2023).
mur_2023.note: Gene-specific ACMG/AMP classification recommendations for the POLE/POLD1 exonuclease domain. KEY POINT: the cancer-predisposing variants are NON-DISRUPTIVE (missense) variants that impair proofreading while keeping polymerase activity. Generic null-variant logic (PVS1) generally does NOT apply to POLE for PPAP.

n363k_2014.citation: A mutation in POLE predisposing to a multi-tumour phenotype (2014).
n363k_2014.note: Germline N363K (c.1089C>A); multi-tumour PPAP (CRC + endometrial/ovarian/brain); residue directly involved in DNA binding.

lindsay_v411l_2017.citation: Novel germline POLE p.(Val411Leu) causing early-onset cancer-prone syndrome (2017).
lindsay_v411l_2017.note: Germline V411L; severe, early-onset PPAP; CMMRD-mimicking skin features.

han_ec_2022.citation: Characterization of hotspot exonuclease domain mutations in POLE in endometrial cancer (2022).
han_ec_2022.note: Somatic EC hotspots: P286R and V411L most common, then S297F, A456P, S459F; R375Q and P452L characterised as additional deleterious hotspots.

project_schematic.title: 3D-POLE-Modeler current procedural-schematic values
project_schematic.note: Illustrative sub-domain boundaries from the existing viewer. Retained as placeholders ONLY; replace with 9F6D-derived (DSSP) boundaries.

domains.label: N-terminal domain
domains.description: Structural scaffold preceding the catalytic core; bridges the exonuclease and fingers subdomains in the assembled core.
domains._note: End is set to just before the exonuclease region (223). Derive the precise N/C boundaries from 9F6D.

domains.label: 3'->5' exonuclease (proofreading) domain
domains.description: DEDDy-superfamily proofreading domain that excises misincorporated nucleotides using two divalent metal ions. PRIMARY site of cancer-associated POLE variants (somatic ultramutator hotspots and germline PPAP variants).
domains._note: Range 223-517 per Atlas of Genetics (human). Cross-check exact bounds against the UniProt '3'-5' exonuclease' region feature when the release is pinned.

domains.label: Palm subdomain
domains.description: Houses the polymerase active site; conserved palm aspartates (Motif A, Motif C) coordinate two Mg2+ ions for phosphodiester bond formation (two-metal-ion mechanism).
domains._note: Boundaries are illustrative. The palm is discontinuous in sequence (the P-domain is inserted within it). Replace with 9F6D-derived ranges and represent the insertion explicitly.

domains.label: Processivity (P) domain
domains.description: Pol epsilon-specific insertion within the polymerase core that distinguishes Pol epsilon from other B-family polymerases; its base coordinates the [4Fe-4S] cluster via the CysX motif (see metal_sites).
domains._note: Render as a nested insertion within the palm rather than a separate co-linear segment. Human CysX cysteine positions must be mapped from yeast (Sc C665/C667/C668/C763) onto Q07864 and verified.

domains.label: Fingers subdomain
domains.description: Predominantly alpha-helical; the O-helix closes upon correct dNTP binding (open -> ajar -> closed), enforcing Watson-Crick geometry. Rate-limiting step for incorporation; structural basis of replicative fidelity.
domains._note: Confirm which PDB in the 9F6D/E/F set corresponds to open vs ajar vs closed, and tie any quantitative closure values (angle/displacement) to the specific measurement in Roske & Yeeles 2024 rather than presenting them as exact.

domains.label: Thumb subdomain
domains.description: Helical bundle that grips the minor groove of the nascent duplex; contributes to the PCNA interaction surface.

domains.label: POPS (Pol2-family-specific peripheral subdomain)
domains.description: A ~68-residue region at the periphery of the catalytic core, unique to Pol2-family enzymes (~71% identity yeast<->human), implicated in replisome assembly and strand synthesis independent of mutation rate.
domains._note: Optional/advanced annotation. Map the human residue range from the defining study and 9F6D before display.

domains.label: Inactive Pol module (Pol2 module)
domains.description: Non-catalytic B-family polymerase fold that retains the palm/fingers/thumb architecture but has lost enzymatic activity; structural platform bridging the catalytic half to the CTD.
domains._note: Approximate bounds; refine from Yuan 2020 / holoenzyme coordinates.

domains.label: C-terminal domain
domains.description: Contains the CysA and CysB cysteine-rich zinc-finger motifs; platform for binding accessory subunits POLE2/3/4 to complete the heterotetrameric holoenzyme. CysA is required for PCNA binding.
domains._note: Approximate start. The human p59-p261(2142-2286) crystal localises the CysA/CysB region near the C-terminus.

residues.role: Non-catalytic structural proline adjacent to ExoI; site of the most frequent cancer-associated substitution (P286R)
residues._note: The current viewer labels P286 as 'in the ExoII motif'. Literature more often describes it as immediately adjacent to ExoI (which holds D275/E277). Confirm the motif label against the source you adopt.

residues.role: DNA-binding residue in the exonuclease domain; germline N363K is pathogenic (multi-tumour PPAP)
Exonuclease catalytic / DNA-binding aspartate; germline D368V is pathogenic
residues._note: Motif assignment (ExoII vs ExoIII) to be confirmed against UniProt active-site features.

ESTIMATE ONLY (yeast D477 minus the ~15-residue offset). The current viewer's 'D370' for the third exonuclease catalytic residue appears inconsistent with the yeast Asp477 position (expected ~D462 in human). VERIFY the full DEDDy set (ExoI D/E, ExoII D, ExoIII D + the conserved Tyr) against the UniProt Q07864 active-site annotation before display.

residues.role: Polymerase catalytic aspartate, Motif A (metal A/B coordination)
residues._note: Human number not independently verified in this draft. The current viewer also lists a paired D642. Confirm both against UniProt active-site features / 9F6D.

residues.role: Polymerase catalytic aspartate, Motif C (YGDTDS / DTD)
residues._note: Human number not independently verified in this draft. Confirm the DTD aspartates against UniProt / 9F6D.

metal_sites.label: Polymerase two-metal-ion active site
metal_sites.mechanism: Metal A lowers the pKa of the primer 3'-OH nucleophile; metal B coordinates the dNTP beta,gamma-phosphates and stabilises the transition state.
metal_sites._note: Mechanism is well established; the specific human coordinating-residue numbers are flagged 'todo' (see residues).

metal_sites.label: Exonuclease two-metal proofreading site
metal_sites._note: ExoI residues verified; the full carboxylate set inherits the verification flags of its member residues.

metal_sites.label: [4Fe-4S] cluster

annotations.location: CysX motif at the base of the processivity (P) domain, within the catalytic core
annotations.function: Required for template:primer DNA binding; cluster-coordinating Cys mutations abolish DNA binding, polymerase and exonuclease activity.

annotations.location: CysB motif in the C-terminal domain (CTD)
annotations.function: Originally proposed cluster site; required for accessory-subunit complex formation. Structural studies of the human p59-p261 subcomplex show Zn (not Fe-S) in CysA and CysB.

metal_sites._note: Record BOTH annotations and surface the discrepancy in the UI. The catalytic-core functional cluster is the CysX/P-domain one; CysA/CysB in the CTD bind Zn in the human crystal. UniProt's entry text still attributes the cluster to CysB.

metal_sites.label: Zinc finger - CysA motif (CTD)
metal_sites._note: Residue positions to be pulled from UniProt zinc-finger / binding-site features when the release is pinned.

metal_sites.label: Zinc finger - CysB motif (CTD)
metal_sites._note: See fe4s4_cluster for the historical Fe-S-vs-Zn assignment at CysB.

variants.somatic_role: Most frequent somatic POLE exonuclease-domain hotspot; oncogenic; ultramutator phenotype (TMB often >100 mut/Mb) in colorectal and endometrial carcinoma.
variants.mechanism: Exceptionally strong mutator via a fidelity defect DISTINCT from simple loss of proofreading (~50x stronger than exonuclease-null); R286 blocks nascent-strand access to the exo site and yields a less-discriminating polymerase. Residual exonuclease activity (~20-60% WT) is retained.
variants._note: GERMLINE classification (VUS - rarely seen in the germline of affected individuals) is intentionally different from its SOMATIC oncogenic significance. Keep the two axes separate in the UI; do not display 'VUS' next to its somatic-hotspot role without that distinction.

variants.somatic_role: Proofreading-impairing; associated tumours show SBS10 contribution.
variants.mechanism: Germline exonuclease-domain variant impairing proofreading; the single most recurrent PPAP variant (reported in roughly half of PPAP families).

variants.somatic_role: Second most common somatic POLE exonuclease-domain hotspot after P286R (endometrial/colorectal).
variants.mechanism: Recurrent somatic ultramutator EDM; also reported as a germline variant causing severe, early-onset PPAP.
variants._note: cDNA/protein verified; fetch the aggregate VCV id + current germline/oncogenicity classifications at build (a Glioblastoma RCV exists).

variants.mechanism: Germline exonuclease-domain variant; drastically impairs proofreading; DNA-binding residue. Broad multi-tumour PPAP spectrum (CRC plus endometrial/ovarian/brain).
ExoI-region variant; proofreading-impairing. Reported both somatically and as germline (PPAP / Lynch-like).
variants._note: Protein change well established; the c.890C>T cDNA and VCV must be confirmed in ClinVar at build.

variants.somatic_role: Characterised deleterious somatic exonuclease-domain hotspot in endometrial cancer.
Characterised deleterious somatic exonuclease-domain hotspot in endometrial cancer.

variants.mechanism: Germline PPAP variant at an exonuclease catalytic/DNA-binding aspartate (see residue exo_D368).
Germline PPAP variant adjacent to the ExoII catalytic residue D368.
Found both as a somatic variant in hypermutated tumours and as a germline PPAP variant; also reported in a CMMRD-like presentation.

variants.somatic_role: Recurrent somatic exonuclease-domain hotspot in endometrial cancer.

variants.mechanism: Exonuclease-domain variant; reported both somatically and as germline PPAP.

variants.somatic_role: Recurrent somatic exonuclease-domain hotspot (endometrial); Tier I somatic.

interpretive_notes: DIRECTIONALITY (most important): POLE cancer predisposition is NOT loss-of-function. The pathogenic PPAP variants are non-disruptive missense substitutions in the exonuclease domain that impair proofreading while the polymerase stays active. Truncating / frameshift / nonsense (null) POLE alleles are a DIFFERENT disease axis - biallelic hypomorphic/LOF variants cause the recessive syndromes FILS and IMAGEI - and should not be presented alongside the PPAP hotspots as if they share a mechanism. (mur_2023)

ACMG: generic PVS1 ('null variant => pathogenic') should generally NOT be applied to POLE for PPAP, precisely because LOF is not the PPAP mechanism. Use the POLE/POLD1 gene-specific exonuclease-domain criteria. (mur_2023)

Any specific truncating entry (e.g. a 'c.138del / p.Leu46Phefs*8' record) requires a ClinVar VCV id and/or a primary publication before inclusion, plus an explicit label of its mechanism axis (LOF / FILS-IMAGEI), not the PPAP axis. It is omitted from this draft pending that provenance.

TWO CLASSIFICATION AXES: ClinVar now carries a germline classification AND an oncogenicity/somatic classification. P286R is the clearest case where these diverge (germline VUS vs somatic oncogenic hotspot). The viewer should show both and never collapse them.
P286R mechanism: model it as a gain-of-function-like ultramutator (fidelity defect, ~50x exonuclease-null), distinct from a clean proofreading knockout. (kane_shcherbakova_2014, parkash_2019)
Species/numbering: yeast Pol2 residue numbers must be converted before use in human-labelled fields (see meta._numbering_warning).
verification_todo: Pin a UniProt release and re-pull the Q07864 active-site, binding-site, zinc-finger and region features in machine-readable form; replace every residue marked 'todo'/'estimated' and confirm the exonuclease domain bounds.
Resolve the third exonuclease catalytic aspartate (current viewer 'D370' vs homology estimate ~D462) and the full DEDDy carboxylate + conserved Tyr set against UniProt.
Derive palm/fingers/thumb/P-domain boundaries from PDB 9F6D coordinates via DSSP (replace all 'schematic' domain ranges) and map the human CysX cysteines.
Confirm which of 9F6D / 9F6E / 9F6F is the open / ajar / closed fingers state, and attach exact conformational values (angle, displacement) to their source measurement.

For every variant with clinvar_variation_id = null: fetch the aggregate VCV id, the germline classification, the oncogenicity classification, the review status (star rating), and the dbSNP rs id from a dated ClinVar snapshot; verify each missing hgvs_c via VariantValidator/Mutalyzer against NM_006231.4.
Record the ClinVar snapshot date and the COSMIC version (for any SBS10a/SBS10b signature panel) in meta.
